objections.claim: `loadWeave` does not validate locator identity. In the endpoint loop it retains only `loc.kind` in `nodes`, ignores the locator payload, and never verifies that the bare content-address `from_node`/`to_node` is the hash of the corresponding locator. Two different locators of the same kind can therefore be conflated under one ID, and one locator can be split across multiple IDs, producing a graph that does not represent the implied nodes in the ledger.

The claimed fail-closed ledger structure is much weaker than the actual envelope contract. The first line may contain a header plus unrelated edge/trailer fields and those fields are silently ignored; the last line may be `{ "clotho_weave_trailer": null }` or contain edge fields and is accepted as a trailer. Header `weave_version` is not checked, so an incompatible future format can be interpreted with the current registry and metric semantics. Signed edge-record fields are not even structurally required. This is separate from cryptographically verifying the acknowledged hash/signature chain.
objections.scope: ingest.mjs: first-record branch, `clotho_weave_trailer !== undefined` branch, and final header/trailer checks

objections.claim: The path is not actually constrained under `rootDir`: `path.join(rootDir, manifest.snapshot_path)` permits `../` traversal, and no `realpath` containment check prevents an in-root symlink from resolving outside the root. Thus the comment that the manifest binds a location under `rootDir` is false.

The digest pin is only relative to caller-supplied values: both `manifest` and `rootDir` are parameters, and this module authenticates neither. A caller can select arbitrary bytes and supply their matching digest. Unless every call site independently anchors the committed manifest and root, `loadWeave` provides consistency with caller input, not a trust root.

The reader claims canonical JSON-lines and fail-closed anomaly handling but broadens the accepted language with `split("\n").filter(...)` and ordinary `JSON.parse`. It accepts blank lines in any position, CRLF/noncanonical formatting, duplicate JSON keys, missing final newline, and malformed UTF-8 that `Buffer.toString("utf8")` replaces with U+FFFD. It also reports compressed rather than physical line numbers after ignored blank lines.
objections.scope: ingest.mjs: line decoding and parsing in `loadWeave`

objections.claim: All metric entry points silently measure nonexistent or malformed node IDs. `dependencies` and `blastRadius` return empty sets, `relevance` returns zero, and default `riskClass`/`assess` then reports a plausible `medium` result because of the coverage floor. For a loaded weave, absence should be rejected or represented explicitly rather than presented as a measured node. The synthetic tests conceal this because every fixture has an empty `nodes` map.

Finite blast depth is not validated as a nonnegative integer or `Infinity`. `reverseToDepth` relies on JavaScript coercion, so values such as `"2"`, `null`, `NaN`, negative numbers, and fractional depths silently produce inconsistent effective hop limits rather than failing closed.

The risk-threshold oracle does not test blast contribution at all. The blast-10 node is also the maximum-relevance node and is high even if blast is ignored; the blast-3 node has relevance 0.3 and becomes medium solely from the unverified-coverage floor; the blast-2 assertion only says it is not high. An implementation that completely ignores blast radius when choosing the class would pass every current risk assertion.
objections.scope: scripts/test-metrics.mjs: `blast thresholds` fixtures and risk-class integration

objections.claim: The relevance class cutoffs are not pinned as claimed. Values 0.75 and 0.5 only constrain the high cutoff to a broad interval and do not test inclusivity at 0.66. The 0.33 medium cutoff is completely masked by the default unverified-coverage floor, so removing the relevance-medium branch can still pass. Tests need attested-complete cases immediately below, at, and above both thresholds.
objections.scope: scripts/test-metrics.mjs: `relevance DRIVES the class` and coverage fixtures

objections.claim: There is no end-to-end oracle for ingestion field mapping. Metric direction tests construct already-normalized `{from,to,edge_kind}` objects and bypass `loadWeave`; the positive ingestion test checks only edge count. The real smoke test derives its hub and expected inequalities from the same loaded edges, while `max relevance == 1` is largely a consequence of normalization. A loader that systematically swaps `from_node` and `to_node`, drops locator data, or otherwise remaps records can remain self-consistent and escape these checks. Fixed expected node IDs, edge tuples, closures, and risk values from the committed snapshot are needed.
objections.scope: scripts/test-metrics.mjs: separation between synthetic metric tests, ingestion positive test, and real-snapshot smoke test

objections.claim: The package-boundary oracle is bypassable within its claimed static-import profile. Its `from` regex stops at a newline, so a legal multiline import such as `import { x }\nfrom "../../clotho/query.mjs"` is not detected. It also scans only `ingest.mjs` and `measure.mjs`; either can import an allowed in-package helper whose own Clotho import is never scanned. Lexical `path.resolve` containment likewise does not account for symlinked modules. Consequently a future runtime can import the Clotho spine while this test remains green.
objections.scope: scripts/test-boundary.mjs: `violations` static-import regexes and fixed two-file scan

objections.claim: Comment claims the manifest binds snapshot LOCATION under rootDir, but path.join(rootDir, manifest.snapshot_path) does not resolve+verify containment: an absolute snapshot_path or ../ segments escape rootDir (e.g. path.join(root,'/etc/passwd') => '/etc/passwd'). Digest still must match, yet the advertised location binding is not enforced.

Real-snapshot checks are near-tautologies for many wrong implementations: max relevance == 1.0 holds for any raw/maxRaw normalization with ≥1 metric edge; hubBlast >= direct depends-on in-degree holds for any reverse closure that includes immediate predecessors; class ∈ {low,medium,high} and edge count alone never pin a golden node id → {dependencies, blast membership, relevance, class} against the committed weave.

depends-on direction is documented and oracled; verified-by and introduced-by only contribute as in-edges to e.to with no Clotho-registry direction pin and no real-snapshot assertion about which endpoint accrues weight. If the committed weave orients those kinds opposite this assumption, relevance and riskClass are systematically wrong while all synthetic fixtures still pass.

byBlast uses absolute cutovers (3/10) on unnormalized reverse-closure size. On a ~4001-edge weave this readily saturates medium/high and loses discrimination; smoke never asserts hub class, class histogram, or that thresholds remain meaningful at real scale.

Default coverage is "unverified" and no attestation resolver exists (stated partial). Every production assess/riskClass path therefore cannot return class "low" (floor to medium). Tests still treat attested-complete low as live metric semantics; the effective advisory scale on real calls is {medium,high} without being pinned on the real snapshot.
depth 1/2/full cases only assert .count, not membership. An off-by-one hop implementation or wrong node set with identical cardinality passes the oracle.

"blast 2 -> not high" accepts any non-high (including coverage-floored medium). With default coverage, byBlast low is never observed as class low; the blast ∈ {0,1,2} → low band and the 0.33 byRel boundary are not discriminated under attested-complete.

Loader never requires prev_hash/record_hash/signature (or trailer tallies) to be present—only edge_kind/endpoints/locators. Any digest-pinned JSONL with header/edges/trailer shape is measured, including chain-broken or signature-stripped bytes; trust reduces to raw digest + partial schema despite "signed EDGE" framing.

ingest exports METRIC_EDGE_KINDS but measure hardcodes "depends-on" branches and RELEVANCE_WEIGHTS keys separately; no runtime/test coupling prevents drift (e.g. adding a metric kind in one place only). Non-contribution of documented-in/discharges/supersedes to dependencies() is not oracled.

Duplicate detection key is only edge_kind|from_node|to_node; parallel same-kind edges that differ only by source_ref/provenance are rejected. Valid multi-provenance weaves would fail-closed (not hit by current smoke snapshot).
JSON null/non-object middle lines skip header/trailer guards then throw TypeError on r.edge_kind access instead of the documented fail-closed Error paths covered by per-branch negatives.

Ingest validates locator kinds into weave.nodes but all metrics ignore nodes/kinds and treat every bare-hex id uniformly; kind-confused or cross-domain depends-on edges affect blast/dependencies/relevance equally with no semantic filter.
